title: Define CKC folder and source-ref authority
operator_request: Capture the folder/source provenance authority gap found during MT-010: CKC folder rows currently behave like generic refs, but models need deterministic folder/source meaning for albums and image mining.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by locating existing Loom/assets/source evidence authority first. Do not invent a second CKC-only folder database unless no existing authority exists.

scope.summary: Make CKC folder/source provenance typed, validated, documented, and reusable across image mining, albums, LoRA review, and downstream CUI workflows.
base_scope: Identify the canonical existing Handshake authority for folder refs, source paths, source URLs, and source evidence.
Define helper functions or types for CKC folder/source refs instead of ad hoc string refs.
Validate link-scoped source_path_ref/source_url_ref inputs before mutating album membership.
Expose structured folder/source readouts and drag refs with stable refKind values.
Document how models should use folder/source refs for dataset mining, CKC albums, and CUI-ready assets.
acceptance_criteria: CKC folder/source refs are backed by documented existing Handshake authority or an explicitly added authority layer.
Invalid source refs fail before album mutation and leave album/media state unchanged.
The same media asset can belong to multiple albums with different link-scoped source refs without cross-contamination.
Argus-visible folder/source rows expose typed draggable refs and structured values.
Manual text explains source_path_ref versus source_url_ref and global asset provenance versus per-link provenance.
risks_and_controls: Risk: raw folder strings drift after project relocation. Control: use portable refs/configurable roots rather than hardcoded paths.
Risk: invalid provenance mutates album membership before failing. Control: transaction/rollback tests around invalid refs.
Risk: source refs duplicate existing source-evidence authority. Control: research existing modules first and reuse them.
verification: Backend validation/rollback tests for invalid source refs.
Frontend structured Argus readout tests for folder/source refs.

handoff.note: Created from MT-010 review finding that folder refs are generic provenance refs without a clear CKC authority contract.
